securityWall.summary: no secrets, 0 SAST findings, deps clean

designAudit.notes: matched design tokens; fixed 2 spacing regressions during the audit

_field_notes.operator-disabled gates: When gatesConfig.<gate> is false for the run, that gate's slot above is { status: 'skipped', reason: 'operator-disabled' } instead of a run result (see _gate_slot_when_operator_disabled). It prints 'GATE <name>: skipped (operator-disabled)', never sends the slice back to implement, is excluded from the passed check, and renders 'off' in the terminal verdict. Any gate slot may take this shape: e2e/tests, adversarialReview, adversarialTest, designAudit, video.
_field_notes.kind-conditional gates: A gate skipped because it does not apply to this slice's kind takes { status: 'skipped', reason: 'kind-conditional' } (see _gate_slot_when_kind_conditional). designAudit + video (walkthrough) run for kind ui/mixed only; api slices keep asciinema evidence and no design audit. adversarialTest runs per-slice for ui/mixed; for api slices it runs as ONE batched run-level pass over the API surface (recorded run-level, not here). A kind-conditional skip is visible, never silent, and is excluded from the passed check for that slice.
_field_notes.profile-skipped gates: A gate skipped because the run's profile does not include it takes { status: 'skipped', reason: 'profile:<name>' } (see _gate_slot_when_profile_skipped). The floor never lowers: no profile skips typecheck/lint/structural greps, the securityWall (secrets scan), or committed tests, and any diff touching security-boundary code gets the fresh-context review regardless of profile. Visible, never silent.
_field_notes.profile: patch | feature | build. Assigned at planning (Phase 1 / autothing-plan) by the same sizing that derives the turn cap; operator flags override. Recorded in RUN_LOG RUN-START + evidence-index.gatesConfig; the terminal verdict carries a profile:<name> token. A run may escalate its profile mid-run (patch -> feature -> build) if the work is larger than the label assumed (logged as a DECISION); big work never ships through a small pipeline.
_field_notes.phase: explore | test | gate | evidence | done | blocked  (resume reads this)
_field_notes.kind: ui | api | mixed  (drives testing strategy, kind-conditional gates, and walkthrough flow selection). ui/mixed get design audit + walkthrough + per-slice independent test; api slices keep asciinema evidence, no design audit, and a batched run-level independent-test pass.
_field_notes.gates.<objective>.exit: process exit code; 0 == pass. The /goal evaluator can confirm gates from these without re-running them.
_field_notes.gates.<gate>.durationMs: wall-clock duration of that gate step, in ms (Part 12.1 run accounting). Recorded so the next pipeline-consolidation round is decided from data, not judgment. Token counts are NOT reliably readable in-session and are never fabricated here — duration + model only.
_field_notes.gates.securityWall: the universal deterministic security wall (Part 12.5): gitleaks (secrets) + semgrep (SAST) + a dependency-audit severity check, installed by the foundation/preflight, run BEFORE any model gate (Part 8.3 deterministic-wall-first), zero tokens. A non-zero exit / high-or-critical finding sends the slice back to autothing-implement like any other correctness failure. Project-specific Semgrep rules are the extension point fed by the determinism ratchet (accepted findings become rules).
_field_notes.gates.adversarialReview.verdict: approve | needs-work. THE per-slice code review (Part 8.1 merged the old same-model autothing-review into this one). A FRESH-CONTEXT Anthropic reviewer (no access to the implementer's notes/rationale — see autothing-adversarial-review), Fable-pinned, evidence-mandatory: it runs build/typecheck/lint/tests itself and every finding cites a spec/acceptance section or attaches failing output. Decorrelation here comes from context, not vendor — this is not a Codex call (Codex now runs once, run-level, as autothing-codex-checkpoint). 'needs-work' sends the slice back to autothing-implement; re-review after the fix, bounded by the slice's own 5-attempt ceiling.
_field_notes.gates.adversarial*.by: the fresh-context Anthropic model actually used (e.g. claude-fable-5 for review, claude-sonnet-5 for test), for auditing.
_field_notes.gates.adversarial*.actualModel / .fallba: Part 3.1 model honesty. actualModel is the model the gate ACTUALLY ran on; when it differs from the pinned model, fallbackCause names why ('classifier-redirect' | 'capacity' | 'limit' | 'credit'). A model-fallback also emits a DECISION entry in RUN_LOG and adds a model-fallbacks:<n> token to the terminal verdict when n>0. A degraded review is recorded as degraded (e.g. 'degraded (classifier-redirect)'), never presented as the pinned bar. Retry policy: classifier-redirect -> retry the pinned model ONCE immediately; capacity (e.g. 529) -> retry ONCE after a 2-5 min backoff; one retry maximum, then accept + record.
_field_notes.gates.adversarialTest.result: pass | fail  (an independent, fresh-context Anthropic session — no diff, no committed test — independently drove the running app through the acceptance with its OWN self-written Playwright probes; see autothing-adversarial-test). A fail must carry a reproducible probe (gates.adversarialTest.probe), never just an impression. For api-kind slices this runs as a batched run-level pass, recorded in evidence-index, and the per-slice slot is { status:'skipped', reason:'kind-conditional' }.
_field_notes.gates.codexSliceReview: The CONDITIONAL per-slice CROSS-MODEL Codex adversarial pass (reintroduced from run 20260706 evidence — per-slice Codex caught real Criticals) that runs ALONGSIDE adversarialReview, distinct from the run-level codexCheckpoint. verdict: approve | needs-work. by/actualModel: gpt-5.5, effort: high. ONE serial `codex exec`, diff-scoped narrow rubric (slice invariants + acceptance + diff, 'find violations'). Runs for profile=build always, profile=feature only on security-boundary slices, NEVER profile=patch; --no-codex-slice forces it off. A slice the conditional does not select → { status:'skipped', reason:'profile-conditional' }; operator-disabled → { status:'skipped', reason:'operator-disabled' }. CREDIT/AVAILABILITY DEATH: a codex exec quota/auth/availability failure → { status:'degraded', reason:'codex-unavailable', actualModel:null } — emit the mid-run notification, log a DECISION, CONTINUE; a degraded slot never blocks the slice, never fakes an approve (the fresh-context adversarialReview still stands), and is excluded from the codexSlice:<approved>/<ran> verdict token. needs-work → back to autothing-implement within the slice's existing 5-attempt ceiling. Auth via API KEY (not ChatGPT sign-in) + budget cap — see autothing-codex-checkpoint/references/codex-checkpoint.md.
_field_notes.gates.designAudit.verdict: clean | issues  (subjective gate reduced to a written verdict + who produced it). Runs for ui/mixed only; api slices carry { status:'skipped', reason:'kind-conditional' } here.
_field_notes.gates.video.status: verified | failed-but-unblocking | skipped. 'failed-but-unblocking' = walkthrough hit its retry ceiling (STUCK); log a blocker and CONTINUE, never wait for input. 'evidence-degraded' (recorded on the video slot) = evidence-mode walkthrough hit its 1-record ceiling on a caption/legibility ground while its functional assert passed — an honest degraded artifact, not a re-record chase.
_field_notes.gates.video.mode: marketing | evidence. Under autothing the walkthrough always runs mode:evidence (see autothing-walkthrough): one beat per acceptance criterion, short holds, terse captions, per-beat vision + inline asserts, NO holistic claude-video gate. Standalone walkthrough defaults to marketing.
_field_notes.gates.video.evidence: path to the evidence.json the evidence-mode walkthrough emits ([{beat, criterionId, timestamp, assert, result}]) — reviews verify against it without watching the video; also referenced from evidence-index.
_field_notes.gates.video.flagged: true if the run is an honest failure (the video shows it not working). An honestly-flagged run still records evidence; it does not fake success.
_field_notes.codexCheckpoint (NOT in this per-slice f: the genuine cross-model Codex check is now RUN-LEVEL, not per-slice — see evidence-index.json's globalGate.codexCheckpoint (autothing-codex-checkpoint), run once in the final phase after the built-in security review.
